speaker: BEDFORD
text: Hung be the heavens with black, yield day to night!

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Comets, importing change of times and states,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Brandish your crystal tresses in the sky,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: And with them scourge the bad revolting stars

speaker: BEDFORD
text: That have consented unto Henry’s death:

speaker: BEDFORD
text: King Henry the Fifth, too famous to live long.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: England ne’er lost a king of so much worth.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: England ne’er had a king until his time.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Virtue he had, deserving to command;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His brandished sword did blind men with his beams;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His arms spread wider than a dragon’s wings;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His sparkling eyes, replete with wrathful fire,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: More dazzled and drove back his enemies

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Than midday sun fierce bent against their faces.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What should I say? His deeds exceed all speech.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He ne’er lift up his hand but conquerèd.

speaker: EXETER
text: We mourn in black; why mourn we not in blood?

speaker: EXETER
text: Henry is dead and never shall revive.

speaker: EXETER
text: Upon a wooden coffin we attend,

speaker: EXETER
text: And Death’s dishonorable victory

speaker: EXETER
text: We with our stately presence glorify,

speaker: EXETER
text: Like captives bound to a triumphant car.

speaker: EXETER
text: What? Shall we curse the planets of mishap

speaker: EXETER
text: That plotted thus our glory’s overthrow?

speaker: EXETER
text: Or shall we think the subtle-witted French

speaker: EXETER
text: Conjurers and sorcerers, that, afraid of him,

speaker: EXETER
text: By magic verses have contrived his end?

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: He was a king blest of the King of kings;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Unto the French the dreadful Judgment Day

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: So dreadful will not be as was his sight.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: The battles of the Lord of Hosts he fought;

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: The Church’s prayers made him so prosperous.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The Church? Where is it? Had not churchmen prayed,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: His thread of life had not so soon decayed.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: None do you like but an effeminate prince

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Whom like a schoolboy you may overawe.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Gloucester, whate’er we like, thou art Protector

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And lookest to command the Prince and realm.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Thy wife is proud; she holdeth thee in awe

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: More than God or religious churchmen may.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Name not religion, for thou lov’st the flesh,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And ne’er throughout the year to church thou go’st,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Except it be to pray against thy foes.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Cease, cease these jars, and rest your minds in peace!

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Let’s to the altar.—Heralds, wait on us.—

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Instead of gold, we’ll offer up our arms,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Since arms avail not, now that Henry’s dead.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Posterity, await for wretched years

speaker: BEDFORD
text: When at their mothers’ moistened eyes babes shall suck,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Our isle be made a nourish of salt tears,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: And none but women left to wail the dead.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Henry the Fifth, thy ghost I invocate:

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Prosper this realm, keep it from civil broils,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Combat with adverse planets in the heavens.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: A far more glorious star thy soul will make

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Than Julius Caesar or bright—

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My honorable lords, health to you all.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Sad tidings bring I to you out of France,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of loss, of slaughter, and discomfiture:

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Guyen, Champaigne, Rheims, Roan, Orleance,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Paris, Gisors, Poitiers, are all quite lost.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: What say’st thou, man, before dead Henry’s corse?

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Speak softly, or the loss of those great towns

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Will make him burst his lead and rise from death.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Is Paris lost? Is Roan yielded up?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: If Henry were recalled to life again,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: These news would cause him once more yield the ghost.

speaker: EXETER
text: How were they lost? What treachery was used?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: No treachery, but want of men and money.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Amongst the soldiers, this is mutterèd:

speaker: MESSENGER
text: That here you maintain several factions

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, whilst a field should be dispatched and fought,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: You are disputing of your generals.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: One would have ling’ring wars with little cost;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Another would fly swift, but wanteth wings;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A third thinks, without expense at all,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: By guileful fair words peace may be obtained.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Awake, awake, English nobility!

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Let not sloth dim your honors new begot.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Cropped are the flower-de-luces in your arms;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Of England’s coat, one half is cut away.

speaker: EXETER
text: Were our tears wanting to this funeral,

speaker: EXETER
text: These tidings would call forth her flowing tides.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Me they concern; regent I am of France.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Give me my steelèd coat, I’ll fight for France.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Away with these disgraceful wailing robes.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Wounds will I lend the French instead of eyes

speaker: BEDFORD
text: To weep their intermissive miseries.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Lords, view these letters, full of bad mischance.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: France is revolted from the English quite,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Except some petty towns of no import.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Dauphin Charles is crownèd king in Rheims;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Bastard of Orleance with him is joined;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: Reignier, Duke of Anjou, doth take his part;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Duke of Alanson flieth to his side.

speaker: EXETER
text: The Dauphin crownèd king? All fly to him?

speaker: EXETER
text: O, whither shall we fly from this reproach?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: We will not fly but to our enemies’ throats.—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Bedford, if thou be slack, I’ll fight it out.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Gloucester, why doubt’st thou of my forwardness?

speaker: BEDFORD
text: An army have I mustered in my thoughts,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Wherewith already France is overrun.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: My gracious lords, to add to your laments,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Wherewith you now bedew King Henry’s hearse,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: I must inform you of a dismal fight

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Betwixt the stout Lord Talbot and the French.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: What? Wherein Talbot overcame, is ’t so?

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: O no, wherein Lord Talbot was o’erthrown.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The circumstance I’ll tell you more at large.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The tenth of August last, this dreadful lord,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Retiring from the siege of Orleance,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Having full scarce six thousand in his troop,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: By three and twenty thousand of the French

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Was round encompassèd and set upon.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: No leisure had he to enrank his men.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: He wanted pikes to set before his archers,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Instead whereof, sharp stakes plucked out of hedges

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: They pitchèd in the ground confusedly

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: To keep the horsemen off from breaking in.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: More than three hours the fight continuèd,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Where valiant Talbot, above human thought,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Enacted wonders with his sword and lance.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Hundreds he sent to hell, and none durst stand him;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Here, there, and everywhere, enraged, he slew.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The French exclaimed the devil was in arms;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: All the whole army stood agazed on him.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: His soldiers, spying his undaunted spirit,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: “À Talbot! À Talbot!” cried out amain

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: And rushed into the bowels of the battle.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Here had the conquest fully been sealed up

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: If Sir John Fastolf had not played the coward.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: He, being in the vaward, placed behind

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: With purpose to relieve and follow them,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Cowardly fled, not having struck one stroke.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Hence grew the general wrack and massacre.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Enclosèd were they with their enemies.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: A base Walloon, to win the Dauphin’s grace,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Thrust Talbot with a spear into the back,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Whom all France, with their chief assembled strength,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Durst not presume to look once in the face.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Is Talbot slain then? I will slay myself

speaker: BEDFORD
text: For living idly here, in pomp and ease,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Whilst such a worthy leader, wanting aid,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Unto his dastard foemen is betrayed.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: O, no, he lives, but is took prisoner,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: And Lord Scales with him, and Lord Hungerford;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Most of the rest slaughtered or took likewise.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: His ransom there is none but I shall pay.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: I’ll hale the Dauphin headlong from his throne;

speaker: BEDFORD
text: His crown shall be the ransom of my friend.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Four of their lords I’ll change for one of ours.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Farewell, my masters; to my task will I.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Bonfires in France forthwith I am to make,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: To keep our great Saint George’s feast withal.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Ten thousand soldiers with me I will take,

speaker: BEDFORD
text: Whose bloody deeds shall make all Europe quake.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: So you had need; ’fore Orleance besieged,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The English army is grown weak and faint;

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: The Earl of Salisbury craveth supply

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: And hardly keeps his men from mutiny,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              MESSENGER
text: Since they so few watch such a multitude.

speaker: EXETER
text: Remember, lords, your oaths to Henry sworn:

speaker: EXETER
text: Either to quell the Dauphin utterly

speaker: EXETER
text: Or bring him in obedience to your yoke.

speaker: BEDFORD
text: I do remember it, and here take my leave

speaker: BEDFORD
text: To go about my preparation.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I’ll to the Tower with all the haste I can

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To view th’ artillery and munition,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And then I will proclaim young Henry king.

speaker: EXETER
text: To Eltham will I, where the young king is,

speaker: EXETER
text: Being ordained his special governor;

speaker: EXETER
text: And for his safety there I’ll best devise.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: Each hath his place and function to attend.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: I am left out; for me nothing remains.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: But long I will not be Jack-out-of-office.

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: The King from Eltham I intend to steal,

speaker: WINCHESTER
text: And sit at chiefest stern of public weal.
